import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import ta

def generate_signals(df, params):
    window = params['window']
    threshold = params['threshold']
    rsi_window = params['rsi_window']
    
    # Calculate rolling mean and standard deviation
    rolling_mean = df['close'].rolling(window=window).mean()
    rolling_std = df['close'].rolling(window=window).std()
    
    # Calculate RSI
    rsi = ta.momentum.RSIIndicator(df['close'], window=rsi_window).rsi()
    
    # Generate signals
    signals = pd.Series(0, index=df.index)
    signals[(df['close'] < rolling_mean - threshold * rolling_std) & (rsi < 30)] = 1
    signals[(df['close'] > rolling_mean + threshold * rolling_std) & (rsi > 70)] = -1
    
    return signals.astype(int)
rationale: XAU/USD exhibits mean-reverting behavior with RSI confirming overbought/oversold conditions at extreme price deviations from the rolling mean.
